analysis.summary: Simple open-source crossover indicator comparing an SMA of close against an EMA of that SMA. Directional state flips when the EMA crosses the MA.
analysis.translationNotes: Original Pine sets pos=1 when xEMA < xMA and pos=-1 when xEMA > xMA. Strategy wrapper preserves that literal directional-state logic instead of imposing a different convention.

failureModes: Risk-adjusted performance may collapse out of sample
Live behavior may be psychologically hard to hold
killCriteria: Reject if drawdown expands materially in broader matrix testing
assessment.recommendedNextStep: Do not promote yet; either improve evidence quality or move on.
strengths: Evidence spans more than one pair
weaknesses: Return profile is still weak or too narrow
Drawdown is too large for easy live confidence

items.author: HPotter
items.notes: Clean baseline validation script. Full source captured from TradingView Monaco view-lines. Translation is close to the original logic: compare SMA(close, n) against EMA(SMA(close, n), n).
items.title: EMA & MA Crossover

analysis.summary: MACD crossover state strategy in the same persistent-state style used by HPotter's older public scripts. Long state when MACD line is above signal line, short state when below.
analysis.translationNotes: TradingView search and script identity were confirmed with the TradingView skill, but the Pine editor stayed pinned to the previously opened script during this session. Translation uses the canonical MACD crossover state logic consistent with the confirmed script identity.

analysis.summary: Visible Pine logic suggests a pivot-high/pivot-low structure model with historical slope calculations and a machine-learning flavored clustering layer that is not fully captured in the current extraction.
analysis.translationNotes: This is not a faithful full Pine port. It is a workflow validation using the visible extracted core ideas: pivot structure plus slope direction.

analysis.summary: Adaptive signal moving average by LuxAlgo using SMA target, correlation-squared weighting, and a persistent directional state to update the synthetic MA.
analysis.translationNotes: Python translation preserves the recursive `ma` and `os` update logic from the Pine source, then uses close-vs-signal-MA crossovers as an explicit backtestable wrapper.

analysis.summary: Simple RSI threshold-state indicator by HPotter. The original Pine keeps a persistent directional state based on RSI > 70 or RSI < 30.
analysis.translationNotes: Original Pine sets pos=1 when RSI > Overbought and pos=-1 when RSI < Oversold. Translation preserves those literal state semantics rather than applying standard mean-reversion assumptions.
